pages.raw_text:
The report of the civic commits
sion was read to the chamber of
commerce at the noon luncheon
today by Secretary Zimmerman.
Theh most important recommends
ton in the report was that re-
ferring to the purchase of civic
center. The commission recon.
mended that the city of Spokane
try to induce congress to appro.
pirate money to purchase a. piece
of ground approximating four
acres west and north of the new
federal building, to be turned into
a civic center. 1t is argued that
the federal building now being
constructed will be inadequate for
the demands on it, even by the
time it is completed, and that it
will be question of a short time
only till further buildings will be
necessary to accommodate the
governments interests in Spo-
kane. With this in view it is be-
lieved that the property can be
purchased now cheaper than will
be possible later.


The chamber did not take any
action on the report today, but
passed a resolution to have the
matter put before the public
through the newspapers of the
city so that the people may be
made thoroughly conversant with
the project before anything is done
about it.


The chamber will hold smoker
in the near future and take action
on the report of the commission
at that time.


The commissions report also
recommends that all streets out
side of the business districts shall
be made not wider than 2 feet,
shall have eight foot parking
strips and at least five foot con-
crete sidewalks; also that all
sewers, watermains, ETC., be laid
in the parking strip so that they
may be repaired without injury to
the pavement. 1t is recommended
that the kind of pavement to be
used, the drainage, etc., be left to
the discretion of the engineer who
lays out the streets.


The report also recommends
that inasmuch as the city charter
is now antiquated and entirely I'M
fit for a city of the size of Spo-
kane, that steps be taken to revise
the city charter
